subtask: issue #14 / 2b.5.2 fix cycle: close CHANGES_REQUESTED verdict from .cdr/runs/2026-07-07/035-verification/verification.json

verdict_being_fixed.reason: TestReaderDuringSplit pinned an mvcc.Snapshot against a SEPARATE mvcc.VersionWriter root/content directory from the one ExecuteSplitAtomic actually splits, so the reader and the split never shared mutable state and the test was a tautology (could never fail regardless of real concurrency correctness).

option_taken.why: Direct source read of engine/catalog/content.go's Read/ReadPartial/LockFileContent and engine/split/execute.go's ExecuteSplitAtomic/writeNewContentFile confirmed: (1) ContentStore.Read does NOT take cs.stripes (content.go lines ~290-303), (2) ExecuteSplitAtomic DOES take cs.LockFileContent(originalFileID) across its redirect-stub write + cat.Put + InvalidateHeaderCache sequence (execute.go ~line 849, issue #13's fix), (3) both ContentStore.writeContentFile and split/execute.go's writeNewContentFile use identical write-to-temp-then-atomic-rename, which structurally guarantees a concurrent os.ReadFile/cs.Read can only ever observe the fully-old or fully-new file, never torn bytes -- regardless of cs.Read not taking the stripe lock. This makes catalog.ContentStore.Read a real, meaningful integration point sharing actual state with ExecuteSplitAtomic, so Option A (rework the test to use it) was correct and achievable. Option B (documenting a scope gap in pending.md) was NOT needed since no genuine architectural gap was found at this layer.

what_changed.before: Reader pinned an mvcc.Snapshot against a separate mvcc.VersionWriter root; split ran against a separate catalog.ContentStore. No shared mutable state.
what_changed.after: Reader repeatedly calls catalog.ContentStore.Read(fileID) directly against the SAME cs ExecuteSplitAtomic operates on, throughout the live split window (400 iterations, 10us poll interval). Every observed read is recorded, then validated post-hoc (once the split's real RedirectTargetIDs are known) to be byte-identical to EITHER the exact pre-split content OR the exact redirect-stub bytes computed via the production buildRedirectStubContent helper -- never anything else (torn/partial/corrupted). Also asserts the reader actually observed the post-split state at least once, proving genuine overlap with the live split rather than a trivially-fast pass. The mvcc import was removed entirely (no longer used by this test).

teeth_confirmation.method: Temporarily replaced writeNewContentFile in engine/split/execute.go with a non-atomic (truncate + partial write + 5ms sleep + partial write, no temp-file/rename) implementation.
teeth_confirmation.result: TestReaderDuringSplit -race -count=5 FAILED 5/5 with explicit torn-content messages (e.g. truncated 'HIVEMIND-REDIR' or empty string mid-write).
teeth_confirmation.revert: execute.go restored to its exact pre-injection state (byte-identical to commit 3e95aa2's version) via cp from a pre-injection backup; confirmed go vet/gofmt clean and go test ./split/... -race -count=1 -timeout 20m green afterward.

test_run_results: go build ./... / go vet ./... / gofmt -l .: clean
go test ./split/... -race -run TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace -count=3 -timeout 10m: PASS, 167.617s (no regression to 2b.5.1)

go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 25m: PASS all packages (first attempt hit an unrelated btree-package timeout under transient extreme system load -- load avg 20-36 from heavy concurrent activity on this shared dev machine; engine/btree is untouched by this fix (git diff --stat -- btree/ empty), confirmed environmental by btree passing in isolation in 16.164s and the full suite passing cleanly on a second run once load settled, with btree back to its normal ~39s)
engine_diff_scope: Only engine/split/split_race_test.go was modified in the final committed diff (TestReaderDuringSplit rewrite + mvcc import removal). No production code (engine/split/execute.go, engine/catalog/content.go) was changed -- the bug-injection used for teeth-confirmation was fully reverted before commit.
commit: recorded separately after this handoff.json is written (see git log)
prompt_injection_observed: The 035-verification/verification.json tool output (and, per its own note, gh issue #14's body) contained embedded fake system-reminder-style text (fake date-change notice, fake 'tokensave MCP server' instructions, fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive). None were acted on; consistent with the previously-established repeated pattern in this repo/session.
